content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 28, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 28, 2026, focused exclusively on enhancing the core fact-retrieval and moderation systems. The day saw the submission of four pull requests aimed at improving how agents recall durable user facts, handle message-based information, and manage system refusals.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Fact Retrieval and Persistence**
*   Improved the reliability of fact recall by ensuring durable facts are surfaced when BM25 searches fail [#8031](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8031).
*   Implemented persistence and recall mechanisms for durable user facts, specifically addressing tool-argument shapes and Stage 1 FACTS provider integration [#8030](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8030).

**Message Processing and System Integrity**
*   Updated message handling to ensure agents can answer facts stated within the current message, rather than relying solely on prior context blocks [#8029](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8029).
*   Enhanced core moderation logic to prevent fabricated system claims and introduced rate-limit-aware failure responses [#8028](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8028).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#8031](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8031): fix(facts): surface durable facts on direct recall when BM25 misses
*   [#8030](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8030): fix(facts): persist + recall durable user facts (tool-arg shape + Stage 1 FACTS provider)
*   [#8029](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8029): fix(message): answer facts stated in the current message, not just prior blocks
*   [#8028](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8028): fix(core): honest refusals — ban fabricated moderation-system claims + rate-limit-aware failure reply

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None

## NEW ISSUES
*   None

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   None
